Developer Notes: - Each "Get Quote →" button should pre-fill the engine code in the quote form and trigger a scroll to the registration input or quote CTA - Implement internal linking structure: each engine code row will eventually link to dedicated engine page (e.g., /chrysler-enr-engine/) — use `rel="nofollow"` until pages are live - Add `data-engine-code="[CODE]"` attributes to each row for analytics tracking and dynamic filtering - Ensure table is responsive: horizontal scroll on mobile or stacked card layout for smaller viewports - Prices must be dynamically updatable via CMS; never hard-code in frontend Brand tone note: Chrysler is a niche, price-sensitive UK brand with problem-led searches; copy emphasizes value comparison, typical market ranges, and warranty reassurance without premium markup language, aligning with KRF priority clusters around "cheap", "compare", and "used/reconditioned" language.

commonProblems.h2: Common Chrysler Engine Problems — What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: Chrysler's most-searched engines in the UK — the 2.8 CRD ENR (Voyager/Grand Voyager), 3.0 CRD EXL (300C), and 2.4L petrol (PT Cruiser) — are prone to specific, well-documented failure patterns that often drive owners toward replacement rather than repair. Every rebuilt or reconditioned Chrysler engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point — plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. The problem groups below are structured as a decision tool: compare realistic repair tiers, understand when replacement offers better long-term value, and get tailored quotes from UK specialists in minutes.

problems.h4: EGR Valve & Turbocharger Failure – Why Chrysler 2.8 CRD Engines Lose Power Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Chrysler Grand Voyager V, Voyager IV (2004–2010, ENR 2.8 CRD diesel)
problems.rootCause: Soot buildup from short journeys and infrequent oil changes clogs the EGR valve and accelerates turbocharger wear; poor-quality diesel exacerbates carbon deposits, restricting airflow and boost pressure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Remove and clean or replace EGR valve; reset adaptation values; road test to confirm boost recovery | Viable only if turbo is still healthy and failure is caught early; does not address underlying carbon buildup elsewhere |

| Remove engine ancillaries, replace turbocharger assembly with quality aftermarket or OEM unit, replace associated gaskets and oil lines | Restores performance if block and internals are sound; requires diligent servicing to prevent repeat failure |

| Supply & fit of fully reconditioned ENR engine with upgraded EGR/turbo components, bench testing, and 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty | Best value when engine has high mileage or secondary damage; eliminates repeat EGR/turbo failures with upgraded parts |

problems.recommendation: On a Grand Voyager worth £1,500–£3,500, if the EGR fault is isolated and the turbo spins freely, the intermediate tier offers sensible value. However, if the engine has exceeded 90,000 miles or shows signs of oil consumption, investing in a reconditioned ENR unit with upgraded components is the more reliable long-term choice — and often costs less than repeated dealer repairs.
problems.cta: → Compare reconditioned ENR 2.8 CRD engine prices for your Grand Voyager

problems.h4: Carbon Build‑up on Intake Valves – The 3.0 CRD EXL's Silent Power Killer
problems.affectedModels: Chrysler 300C (2005–2012, EXL 3.0 CRD diesel, Mercedes‑sourced OM642)
problems.rootCause: Direct‑injection diesel design means fuel does not wash over intake valves; combined with EGR recirculation, carbon accumulates on valve backs, restricting airflow and causing incomplete combustion.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Remove intake manifold, blast intake valves with walnut media to remove carbon, clean manifold and EGR passages | Effective preventative measure if caught before misfires become severe; does not repair damaged valves or seats |

| Remove cylinder head, professionally clean valves and ports, replace valve stem seals and gaskets, reassemble with updated torque specs | Appropriate if valves show pitting or seats are damaged; requires skilled machining and careful reassembly |

| Supply & fit of fully reconditioned EXL engine with updated intake components, bench‑tested, and backed by 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty | Recommended when misfires persist after cleaning or when compression loss indicates internal damage; eliminates carbon‑related repeat failures |

problems.recommendation: For a 300C valued at £2,500–£5,000, walnut blasting is a cost‑effective first step if symptoms are mild and mileage is under 80,000. However, if diagnostic scans show persistent multi‑cylinder misfires or compression loss, a reconditioned EXL engine with upgraded intake components delivers better reliability per pound spent — and avoids the risk of recurring carbon issues.
problems.cta: → Get quotes for rebuilt EXL 3.0 CRD engines with upgraded intake components

problems.h4: Ignition System Misfires – Why PT Cruiser 2.4L Engines Run Rough
problems.affectedModels: Chrysler PT Cruiser (2000–2010, 2.4L petrol EDZ/EDJ), Sebring 2.4L
problems.rootCause: Early 2.4L engines used spark plug designs prone to fouling and electrode wear; combined with ageing ignition coils and wiring, this leads to intermittent misfires, rough idle, and catalytic converter stress.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Replace all four spark plugs with revised OEM‑spec parts, test and replace faulty ignition coils, clear fault codes | Effective if misfires are recent and no catalytic damage has occurred; requires quality parts to prevent rapid recurrence |

| Replace damaged catalytic converter, renew full ignition system (plugs, coils, leads), perform exhaust back‑pressure test | Necessary if misfires have triggered P0420/P0430 codes; restores emissions compliance but does not address engine wear |

| Supply & fit of reconditioned 2.4L engine with updated ignition components, bench‑tested, and covered by 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty | Best option when engine shows low compression, oil consumption, or repeated misfires after ignition repairs; eliminates root cause |

problems.recommendation: On a PT Cruiser worth £800–£2,200, the minor tier is worthwhile if the misfire is recent and compression tests are healthy. However, if the vehicle has exceeded 80,000 miles or shows signs of oil burning, a reconditioned 2.4L engine with updated ignition components offers better long‑term value and avoids the risk of catalytic converter damage from ongoing misfires.
problems.cta: → Compare rebuilt 2.4L engine prices for your PT Cruiser

problems.h4: Camshaft Sensor & Starting Faults – The Intermittent No‑Start That Strands Owners
problems.affectedModels: Chrysler Voyager/Grand Voyager (2004–2010, ENR 2.8 CRD), PT Cruiser (2.4L petrol), 300C (3.0 CRD)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 40,000–80,000 miles (can occur at any mileage due to electrical vulnerability)
problems.rootCause: Camshaft position sensor wiring harnesses are exposed to heat and vibration; connectors corrode or pins loosen, causing intermittent signal loss that prevents ECU from timing fuel injection correctly.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Replace camshaft position sensor, inspect and repair wiring harness connectors, clear fault codes and perform adaptive relearn | Effective if fault is isolated to sensor/connector; requires careful routing to prevent repeat heat damage |

| Replace engine wiring loom section containing sensor circuits, secure new harness away from heat sources, retest all related systems | Appropriate when multiple electrical faults appear or harness shows visible cracking; prevents cascade failures |

repairOptions.tier: | Full Replacement – Reconditioned Engine with Updated Harness |
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Supply & fit of reconditioned engine with updated wiring harness and sensor package, bench‑tested, 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty | Recommended when electrical faults coincide with mechanical wear (high mileage, oil consumption); eliminates both sensor and engine reliability concerns |

problems.recommendation: For any Chrysler model under 70,000 miles with an isolated no‑start fault, the minor tier is a sensible first repair. However, if the vehicle has high mileage or shows additional electrical gremlins, investing in a reconditioned engine with an updated harness delivers greater peace of mind — and often costs less than chasing intermittent faults across multiple systems.
problems.cta: → Request quotes for Chrysler engines with updated sensor packages

problems.h4: Oil Consumption & Lubrication Failure – When Petrol Voyagers Burn Their Way to Replacement
problems.affectedModels: Chrysler Voyager/Grand Voyager (2000–2008, EGM 3.3L/3.8L petrol), Sebring 3.5L V6
problems.rootCause: Worn piston rings and valve stem seals allow oil to enter combustion chambers; combined with infrequent oil changes, this leads to sludge buildup, reduced oil pressure, and accelerated bearing wear.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Replace PCV valve, renew valve cover gasket and external seals, perform oil system flush with quality detergent oil | May reduce consumption if rings are still serviceable; does not repair internal wear or sludge damage |

| Remove oil pan, replace main and rod bearings, renew piston rings, clean oil galleries, reassemble with updated torque specs | Viable if block and crank are undamaged; requires precision machining and strict assembly tolerances to prevent repeat failure |

| Supply & fit of fully reconditioned 3.3L/3.8L engine with upgraded rings, bearings, and oil pump, bench‑tested, 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty | Best value when engine shows low compression, knocking, or excessive smoke; eliminates oil consumption and bearing failure risks |

problems.recommendation: On a Voyager worth £1,200–£3,000, if oil consumption is mild (<1L/1,000 miles) and compression is healthy, the intermediate tier can extend life cost‑effectively. However, if the engine knocks, smokes heavily, or has exceeded 100,000 miles, a reconditioned EGM/EGH unit with upgraded internals is the more reliable investment — and often cheaper than repeated dealer rebuilds.
problems.cta: → Compare prices on reconditioned 3.3L/3.8L Voyager engines

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Chrysler
finalCta.paragraph: Whether your Chrysler is suffering from EGR clogging, carbon‑choked valves, ignition misfires, sensor faults, or oil‑burning wear, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK specialists who supply and fit used, reconditioned, or remanufactured engines. Every engine in our network includes upgraded components targeting the original failure point, bench testing, and a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty — with nationwide delivery or mobile fitting available. Instead of paying main‑dealer premiums for repeated repairs, get tailored quotes in minutes and get back on the road with confidence.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Chrysler Engine Replacement Prices Now
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Chrysler model and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty — always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineTypes.h2: Chrysler Engine Types – Options, Costs & What to Expect
engineTypes.intro: Every Chrysler engine type available through our UK network, with typical price ranges and recommendations based on your vehicle's age, condition and your budget.

types.title: Chrysler Reconditioned Engines (Recon)
types.description: A stripped, cleaned and fully inspected engine where all worn components are replaced with OEM‑spec parts. Best for Chrysler Voyager and 300C owners who want factory reliability without the main dealer price, with upgraded EGR components and injector seals to address common failure points.
types.priceRange: Typical price range: £1,800 – £5,500 (depending on engine code)

types.title: Chrysler Rebuilt Engines
types.description: A complete rebuild with upgraded components addressing known failure points – such as reinforced timing chain tensioners for World Gas 2.4L engines and uprated bearing shells for VM Motori 2.8 CRD units – for a longer service life than a standard replacement.

types.title: Used Chrysler Engines
types.description: A tested, low‑mileage take‑out unit from a donor vehicle. The most affordable option when budget is the priority for PT Cruiser, Voyager or 300C owners, with verified history and warranty available from UK breakers.

types.title: Chrysler Remanufactured Engines
types.description: A factory‑spec, as‑new engine: every surface is machined, every component is replaced, and the unit is built to meet exact OEM tolerances. The premium choice for 300C HEMI V8 or high‑mileage Grand Voyager owners wanting a zero‑mile engine with maximum longevity.

types.title: Chrysler Refurbished Engines
types.description: A lightly serviced, cleaned, and tested engine that works reliably but hasn't had a complete rebuild. Often a pragmatic, fast solution for older PT Cruiser or Ypsilon models where a full recon isn't economical but you need a dependable replacement fast.

types.title: Chrysler Supply & Fit Engines
types.description: Your replacement engine delivered and professionally installed by a UK Chrysler specialist. Includes removal of the old unit, fitting, fluids, and a workmanship warranty – ideal for Voyager and Grand Voyager owners who want a hassle‑free, road‑ready solution.
types.priceRange: Typical added cost: £600 – £1,500 on top of engine price
types.cta: Find Chrysler engine supply & fit deals

engineTypes.closing: All engine types include a minimum 12‑month warranty. Prices are typical UK market ranges and depend on exact engine code, supplier, and any additional fitting services. Use our free comparison tool to get a tailored quote for your Chrysler model today.

engineSizes.h2: Chrysler Engines by Size – Find the Right Displacement for Your Model
engineSizes.intro: Every Chrysler engine size explained — with the models and engine codes that used each displacement — so you can quickly identify the correct replacement unit for your vehicle.

items.title: Chrysler 2.8L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.8L CRD is Chrysler's most-searched diesel displacement in the UK, primarily fitted to the Grand Voyager and Voyager MPVs between 2004–2010. Built on a VM Motori block (engine code ENR), this unit delivers strong torque for people-carrier duties but is prone to EGR clogging and turbocharger wear when servicing intervals are extended — failures that commonly drive owners toward replacement rather than repair. Engine code(s): ENR Compatible models (UK): Grand Voyager IV/V, Voyager (2004–2010) Production years: 2004–2010 Common failure points: EGR valve carbon buildup, turbocharger bearing wear, injector seal leaks
items.cta: Get quotes for Chrysler 2.8L diesel engines

items.title: Chrysler 3.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: Chrysler's 3.0L CRD (Mercedes‑sourced OM642, code EXL) powered the 300C saloon from 2005–2012 and remains a frequent replacement enquiry due to carbon accumulation on intake valves and EGR cooler failures. While robust when maintained, these engines often reach replacement decision points around 90,000–120,000 miles when owners weigh repair costs against a reconditioned unit with upgraded components. Engine code(s): EXL (Mercedes‑Benz OM642) Compatible models (UK): 300C (2005–2012) Production years: 2005–2012 Common failure points: Intake valve carbon buildup, EGR cooler leaks, oil cooler gasket failure
items.cta: Get quotes for Chrysler 3.0L diesel engines

items.title: Chrysler 1.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The compact 1.4L petrol unit (Fiat‑sourced) was fitted to the Chrysler Ypsilon city car for the UK market from 2011–2017. While generally reliable for low‑mileage urban use, these engines can develop ignition coil faults and throttle body issues that, if left unaddressed, lead to misfires and catalytic converter stress — prompting some owners to seek replacement units rather than repeated component repairs. Engine code(s): 312A4000 (Fiat‑derived) Compatible models (UK): Ypsilon (2011–2017) Production years: 2011–2017 Common failure points: Ignition coil failure, throttle body carbon buildup, ECU adaptation faults

items.title: Chrysler 2.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: Chrysler's widely used 2.4L World Engine (codes EDZ/EDJ) powered the PT Cruiser, Sebring, and Voyager from 2000–2010. Known for accessible servicing but susceptible to ignition system wear and oil consumption from piston ring wear on higher‑mileage examples, this displacement frequently appears in replacement enquiries when owners face repeated misfires or smoke-related MOT failures. Engine code(s): EDZ, EDJ Compatible models (UK): PT Cruiser, Sebring, Voyager (2000–2010) Production years: 2000–2010 Common failure points: Ignition coil/spark plug fouling, piston ring wear causing oil consumption, catalytic converter stress from misfires

items.title: Chrysler 2.7L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.7L LH V6 (code EER) appeared in limited‑import Chrysler models such as the 300M and Concorde during the late 1990s–early 2000s. While smooth and refined, this engine is notorious for oil sludge formation when oil change intervals are extended — a failure mode that can lead to bearing wear and low oil pressure, making replacement a more reliable solution than in‑situ repair on high‑mileage examples. Engine code(s): EER Compatible models (UK): 300M, Concorde (limited UK imports, 1999–2004) Production years: 1999–2004 Common failure points: Oil sludge from infrequent changes, timing chain tensioner wear, bearing wear from low oil pressure

items.title: Chrysler 3.3L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 3.3L V6 (code EGM) was the workhorse petrol engine for Chrysler Voyager and Grand Voyager MPVs sold in the UK from 2000–2008. Prone to oil consumption from worn piston rings and valve stem seals on higher‑mileage units, this displacement frequently drives replacement enquiries when owners face blue exhaust smoke, elevated oil top‑up frequency, or failed compression tests. Engine code(s): EGM Compatible models (UK): Voyager, Grand Voyager (2000–2008) Production years: 2000–2008 Common failure points: Piston ring wear causing oil consumption, valve stem seal leakage, head gasket failure on overheated units

items.title: Chrysler 3.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: Chrysler's 3.5L V6 (code EGH) powered the 300C saloon and later Grand Voyager models from 2005–2010, offering smooth V6 refinement but requiring diligent servicing to avoid camshaft phaser wear and oil passage clogging. When neglected, these engines can develop timing-related faults or low oil pressure warnings that make a reconditioned replacement more cost‑effective than repeated top‑end repairs. Engine code(s): EGH Compatible models (UK): 300C, Grand Voyager (2005–2010) Production years: 2005–2010 Common failure points: Camshaft phaser wear, oil passage clogging from sludge, variable valve timing solenoid faults

items.title: Chrysler 3.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The modern 3.6L Pentastar V6 (code ERB) replaced earlier units in the 300C from 2011 onward, delivering improved efficiency and power. Early production builds (pre‑2013) are susceptible to cylinder head casting issues that can cause coolant leaks or misfires, while later units benefit from revised components — making engine code verification essential when sourcing a replacement. Engine code(s): ERB (Pentastar) Compatible models (UK): 300C (2011–2023) Production years: 2011–2023 Common failure points: Cylinder head porosity on pre‑2013 builds, oil filter housing leaks, thermostat housing cracks

engineSizes.closing: Not sure which engine size your Chrysler needs? Enter your registration number and we'll match you to the exact engine code and displacement — instantly. All engines supplied with a minimum 12‑month warranty from UK Chrysler specialists.

fuelTypes.closing: Not sure which fuel type your Chrysler has? Enter your registration number above to instantly identify your model, engine code, fuel system and compatible replacement options – all backed by a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty from UK Chrysler specialists.

engineCodeDirectory.h2: Chrysler Engine Codes – Most Replaced Engines (2008–2025)
engineCodeDirectory.intro: Every major Chrysler engine code from the last two decades, grouped by family, with compatible models and typical reasons why owners seek replacement. Select your engine to compare rebuilt prices and get quotes from UK Chrysler specialists.

entries.title: ENR 2.8L Diesel
entries.description: Powers the Chrysler Grand Voyager IV/V and later Voyager MPVs across the UK market from 2007 to 2016. The most common replacement triggers are severe EGR valve carbon buildup and turbo actuator sticking, which lead to restricted airflow and eventual turbo failure. Quality rebuilt ENR units resolve this by installing upgraded EGR valves, refurbished turbo actuators, and new injector seals to prevent oil dilution and restore reliable towing performance.
entries.cta: Get quotes for ENR Chrysler engine replacement

entries.title: ENS 2.8L Diesel
entries.description: Used in high-spec Grand Voyager RT and RS variants between 2008 and 2011, offering slightly tuned performance over base models. Owners typically seek replacement due to chronic oil pump pressure loss and intercooler pipe cracking under sustained high mileage. A professionally rebuilt ENS engine includes reinforced oil pump pickups, upgraded intercooler pipework, and revised cylinder head gaskets to eliminate recurring pressure warnings.

entries.title: ENT 2.8L Diesel
entries.description: Found in standard Voyager RS models from 2008 to 2016, sharing the same durable VM Motori architecture as its higher-output siblings. Replacement demand is driven primarily by failed swirl flap mechanisms and exhaust manifold stud fractures that cause performance loss and MOT failures. Rebuilt ENT replacements come fitted with revised swirl flap delete components or reinforced flaps, along with new stainless-steel manifold studs for long-term durability.
entries.cta: Find rebuilt ENT Chrysler diesel engines

entries.title: EDJ 2.5L Diesel
entries.description: Featured in earlier Voyager RG generation models (2001–2007) before the transition to the 2.8L block. Premature head gasket failure and camshaft bearing wear are the dominant issues, often compounded by neglected cooling system maintenance. A remanufactured EDJ unit incorporates reinforced multi-layer steel head gaskets, upgraded cam bearings, and a freshly pressure-tested block to restore factory compression and prevent repeat breakdowns.

entries.title: EXL 3.0L Diesel
entries.description: The workhorse of the Chrysler 300C saloon and estate range (2004–2010), delivering strong torque for both executive and fleet use in the UK. Swirl flap linkage wear and oil cooler gasket degradation are well-documented, often allowing oil to contaminate the cooling system. Rebuilt EXL engines address these weaknesses with upgraded swirl components, new oil cooler assemblies, and thoroughly cleaned EGR pathways for sustained reliability.
entries.cta: Get quotes for EXL 3.0 CRD engine replacement

entries.title: EVG 3.0L Diesel
entries.description: Deployed across 300C LE and base trim models from 2005 to 2010, sharing the same robust Mercedes OM642 foundation. Chronic carbon ingress from the EGR system combined with timing chain guide wear frequently leads to rough idling and costly valve train damage. A quality rebuilt EVG unit includes hardened timing chain guides, a comprehensive EGR clean-out or blanking kit, and recalibrated ECU mapping to smooth out idle fluctuations.
entries.cta: Compare EVG Chrysler engine prices online

entries.title: EXW 3.0L Diesel
entries.description: Used in higher-output 300C SRT Design and sport-tuned variants (2006–2010), engineered for enhanced mid-range power. The heightened stress levels accelerate intake manifold runner failures and high-pressure fuel pump wear, making full replacement more economical than piecemeal repairs. Rebuilt EXW replacements feature upgraded intake runner mechanisms, refurbished high-pressure fuel pumps, and reinforced injector seals to maintain optimal combustion efficiency.

entries.title: EXY 3.0L Diesel
entries.description: Installed in 300C Touring estate models (2008–2015), providing durable pulling power for heavy loads across UK motorways. Owners commonly replace these units after water pump impeller failure causes sudden overheating, which can warp cylinder heads if left unchecked. Professionally rebuilt EXY engines include upgraded aluminium water pumps, new thermostat housings, and revised cylinder head studs to withstand heavy touring demands.

entries.title: EDZ 2.4L Petrol
entries.description: The most widely recognised engine in the UK Chrysler lineup, powering the PT Cruiser, Sebring, and early Voyager MPVs from 2000 to 2010. Hydraulic timing chain tensioner collapse is the critical failure point, allowing chain stretch that frequently results in catastrophic valve-to-piston contact. Rebuilt EDZ engines come equipped with uprated hydraulic tensioners, reinforced timing chain guides, and fresh piston ring sets to eliminate rattle and secure long-term timing accuracy.
entries.cta: Get quotes for EDZ 2.4L engine replacement

entries.title: EDT 2.4L Turbo Petrol
entries.description: Found in performance PT Cruiser GT and Sebring trims (2003–2006), delivering boosted output for sportier driving. Turbocharger bearing failure and intercooler piping degradation under high boost are the primary reasons owners seek full engine swaps. A professionally rebuilt EDT unit includes a rebalanced turbocharger assembly, reinforced silicone intercooler hoses, and upgraded head gaskets to safely handle increased cylinder pressures.
entries.cta: Compare EDT turbo petrol engine prices

entries.title: EGR 2.4L Petrol
entries.description: Standard fitment in Voyager RG and early 2000s family MPVs, prioritising smooth low-end torque over outright speed. Persistent oil consumption and intake manifold vacuum leaks eventually starve the upper cylinder walls of lubrication, accelerating bore wear. Rebuilt EGR replacements address this with revised PCV valve systems, upgraded intake manifold gaskets, and machined cylinder bores fitted with new piston assemblies.

entries.title: EDE 2.0L Petrol
entries.description: Utilised in base-model PT Cruiser and Ypsilon hatchbacks (2003–2010) where fuel economy was the primary design focus. Ignition coil pack failures and crankshaft position sensor faults are common, but severe neglect leads to bottom-end bearing spin and block seizure. Quality rebuilt EDE engines include new OEM-spec crankshaft bearings, refreshed sensor wiring harnesses, and upgraded oil pump rotors to prevent sudden stalling.

entries.title: ECH 2.7L V6 Petrol
entries.description: Featured in mid-spec Sebring convertibles and PT Cruiser performance trims (2001–2006), offering a balance of power and refinement. Oil sludge accumulation from extended service intervals is notorious, blocking pickup screens and starving the crankshaft bearings of vital lubrication. A remanufactured ECH unit comes with a deep-cleaned oil gallery system, upgraded high-flow oil pumps, and revised valve cover gaskets to eliminate sludge-related failures.
entries.cta: Get quotes for ECH V6 engine replacement

entries.title: EGM 3.3L Petrol
entries.description: A staple of the Grand Voyager MPV range in the UK from 1998 to 2007, prized for its low-revving torque and towing capability. Oil pump pickup screen clogging remains the Achilles' heel, frequently causing sudden oil starvation and complete bottom-end failure with little warning. Rebuilt EGM engines are fitted with reinforced mesh pickup screens, heavy-duty main bearings, and revised oil pump assemblies to guarantee consistent lubrication.

entries.title: EGL 3.3L Petrol
entries.description: Deployed in long-wheelbase Voyager and commercial-spec derivatives between 2001 and 2007, built for durability under continuous load. Intake manifold gasket leaks and coolant intrusion into the lower cylinders are prevalent, leading to hydro-lock risks and warped block surfaces. Professionally rebuilt EGL replacements include upgraded composite intake gaskets, freshly decked cylinder heads, and reinforced coolant passages to prevent cross-contamination.

entries.title: EGA 3.8L Petrol
entries.description: Reserved for top-spec Grand Voyager IV models (1999–2004), providing additional power for heavier payloads and motorway cruising. Valve guide wear and exhaust manifold cracking under sustained high temperatures often necessitate costly top-end rebuilds that are rarely economical. A quality rebuilt EGA unit features hardened valve guides, stainless-steel exhaust manifolds, and precision-bored cylinders to restore smooth, leak-free operation.

entries.title: EGC 3.8L Petrol
entries.description: Continued in Grand Voyager V and later RT trims (2008–2011), refining the LA architecture for improved emissions compliance. Fuel injector seat degradation and timing cover seal failures lead to external oil leaks and misfires that progressively damage catalytic converters. Rebuilt EGC engines come with reinforced injector seats, upgraded silicone timing seals, and freshly calibrated fuel rails to maintain optimal combustion.

entries.title: EGE 3.8L Petrol
entries.description: Found in late-model Voyager RS MPVs (2008–2016), offering a proven V6 platform with modernised ancillaries. Persistent thermostat housing failures and water pump bearing wear cause intermittent overheating cycles that degrade head gasket integrity over time. Remanufactured EGE units address this with upgraded alloy thermostat housings, heavy-duty water pump impellers, and multi-layer steel head gaskets for lasting thermal stability.

entries.title: EZB 5.7L Petrol
entries.description: The iconic powerplant behind the Chrysler 300C V8 and Commander SUV (2005–2010), celebrated for its muscular torque and distinctive exhaust note. Hydraulic lifter collapse and subsequent camshaft lobe wear are widely reported, particularly when oil change intervals are extended or incorrect viscosity oil is used. Rebuilt EZB HEMI engines feature uprated hydraulic lifters, hardened camshaft profiles, and revised MDS solenoids to eliminate ticking and restore factory performance.
entries.cta: Get quotes for EZB HEMI engine replacement

entries.title: EZH 5.7L Petrol
entries.description: Tuned for SRT8 and performance-oriented 300C variants, pushing higher output through aggressive cam timing and reinforced internals. The increased stress accelerates piston ring land wear and oil consumption, often leading to spark plug fouling and reduced compression. A professionally rebuilt EZH unit includes forged piston assemblies, upgraded oil control rings, and reinforced valve springs to safely manage high-RPM demands.

entries.title: EZL 5.7L Petrol
entries.description: Standard across 300C Touring and luxury V8 estates from 2008 to 2015, balancing power delivery with daily drivability for UK owners. Intake valve carbon buildup and secondary air injection pump failures frequently trigger check-engine warnings and rough cold starts. Quality rebuilt EZL replacements come with chemically cleaned intake ports, refurbished secondary air pumps, and revised PCV systems to ensure consistent cold-start performance.

entries.title: EZM 5.7L Petrol
entries.description: Exclusive to all-wheel-drive 300C V8 variants (2008–2015), engineered with additional oiling capacity and reinforced motor mounts for torque distribution. Transfer case-related driveline shudders can sometimes mask underlying crankshaft bearing wear, which only becomes apparent once oil pressure drops critically. Remanufactured EZM engines include upgraded main and rod bearings, reinforced oil pan baffles, and high-capacity oil filters to maintain stable pressure under AWD load. Closing line (Block A): Can’t find your engine code listed above? Use our full Chrysler engine code directory below — every code from the last 25 years — or enter your registration number for an instant match to the correct replacement engine with warranty.

engineCodeDirectory.closing: Can’t find your engine code listed above? Use our full Chrysler engine code directory below — every code from the last 25 years — or enter your registration number for an instant match to the correct replacement engine with warranty.

directory.h3: Full Chrysler Engine Code Directory (All Years)

directory.intro: A comprehensive alphabetical listing of every Chrysler engine code from the last 35 years. Use the search above or enter your registration for an instant match to your specific engine. All codes link to a quote request.

engineYears.h2: Chrysler Engines by Year – What Was Fitted & When

years.preview: PT Cruiser Launch, Voyager CRD & PowerTech Diesel Introduction 🔴 Major Engine Family Introduction
years.description: Short intro: Chrysler re-entered the mainstream UK market with the retro-styled PT Cruiser and the Grand Voyager MPV, bringing a mix of Chrysler-developed petrol units and Mercedes-sourced diesel technology for the first time. Key Changes PT Cruiser launches in UK with 2.0-litre ECC petrol and 2.2 CRD Mercedes-sourced diesel (OM611-derived) Grand Voyager UK fitment uses 2.5 CRD diesel and 3.3-litre OHV V6 petrol across LX and Limited trim levels Chrysler Neon and Voyager carry over PowerTech 2.4-litre petrol from late 1990s platform 2.5 CRD diesel in Voyager sourced from VM Motori - shared architecture with several European MPV competitors Automatic transmission standard on V6 petrol Grand Voyager; manual available on PT Cruiser diesel Main Engines ECC - 2.0 DOHC four-cylinder petrol (PT Cruiser 2.0) EDZ - 2.4 DOHC four-cylinder petrol (PT Cruiser 2.4, Neon, Voyager 2.4) OM611-derived 2.2 CRD - 2.2 diesel (PT Cruiser 2.2 CRD) VM Motori 2.5 CRD - 2.5 turbo diesel (Grand Voyager 2.5 CRD, Voyager 2.5 CRD) 3.3 OHV V6 - 3.3 petrol (Grand Voyager 3.3, Chrysler Voyager 3.3) Popular Models Chrysler PT Cruiser (2000–2003) Chrysler Grand Voyager (RG - 2001–2003) Chrysler Voyager Chrysler Neon Known For 2.2 CRD in PT Cruiser shares architecture with Mercedes OM611 - well-regarded for longevity but injector seal weeps are common above 90,000 miles VM Motori 2.5 CRD in Voyager is robust at high mileage but EGR valve fouling and swirl flap wear are recurring maintenance items 3.3 OHV V6 petrol is a proven, durable unit with minimal failure points - intake manifold gasket leaks are the most commonly reported fault PT Cruiser's styling generated strong UK sales but rear suspension and underbody corrosion on early examples can complicate high-mileage engine work [Tags] Engine Codes Covered: ECC, EDZ, OM611 (2.2 CRD derivative), VM Motori 2.5 CRD, 3.3 OHV (EGA)
years.cta: Search for 2000–2003 Chrysler replacement engines from UK specialists

years.preview: 300C UK Launch, 5.7 Hemi Introduction & 3.5 V6 Fitment 🔴 Performance Landmark
years.description: Short intro: The Chrysler 300C arrived in the UK as the brand's most significant product launch in decades, bringing the reborn 5.7-litre Hemi V8 and a 3.5-litre V6 to the premium saloon market alongside a 3.0 CRD diesel option for UK buyers. Key Changes Chrysler 300C launched in UK with 5.7 Hemi V8 (EZB) as flagship powertrain - first modern Hemi in a UK-market Chrysler 3.5-litre V6 petrol (EGG) offered as lower-cost 300C entry point for UK market 3.0 CRD V6 diesel (OM642-derived, badged EXL) introduced for UK buyers prioritising economy - same basic architecture as Mercedes E320 CDI PT Cruiser range updated with 2.4-litre EDZ petrol replacing 2.0 ECC as standard petrol unit Crossfire roadster fitted with 3.2-litre Mercedes M112 V6 petrol - cross-brand architecture reflecting DaimlerChrysler merger era Main Engines EZB - 5.7 Hemi V8 (300C, 300C Touring) EGG - 3.5 SOHC V6 petrol (300C 3.5, 300C Touring 3.5) EXL - 3.0 CRD V6 diesel - OM642 derivative (300C 3.0 CRD, 300C Touring CRD) EDZ - 2.4 DOHC four-cylinder petrol (PT Cruiser 2.4) M112 - 3.2 V6 petrol (Crossfire 3.2, sourced from Mercedes) Popular Models Chrysler 300C (LX - 2004–2005) Chrysler 300C Touring Chrysler PT Cruiser (facelift) Chrysler Crossfire Known For 5.7 Hemi V8 delivers a genuinely thrilling driving experience with V8 character rare in UK-market saloons - MDS cylinder deactivation on some variants causes rough idle and solenoid failures above 80,000 miles 3.0 CRD (EXL / OM642) shares Mercedes' swirl flap vulnerability - broken flap debris can cause catastrophic engine damage if pre-purchase inspection is skipped 3.5 V6 EGG is smooth but not a performance unit - throttle body deposits are a known mild issue on higher-mileage examples Crossfire M112 V6 sourced directly from Mercedes inventory - parts availability and reliability broadly mirrors the Mercedes CLK320 of the same era [Tags] Engine Codes Covered: EZB, EGG, EXL, EDZ, M112 (Crossfire), OM642 (CRD)
years.cta: Find 2004–2005 Chrysler 300C Hemi and CRD replacement engines from UK rebuilders

years.preview: Grand Voyager 2.8 CRD, 300C Refresh & Sebring/Avenger Launch 🔴 Major Engine Family Introduction
years.description: Short intro: Chrysler refreshed its UK lineup with an updated Grand Voyager using the VM Motori 2.8 CRD diesel, introduced the Sebring saloon and convertible, and continued 300C production with updated Euro 4-compliant powertrains. Key Changes Grand Voyager updated to 2.8 CRD (VM Motori ENR unit) - replacing the 2.5 CRD across the range Chrysler Sebring launched in UK with 2.0-litre ECC petrol and 2.0 CRD diesel (OM640-derived) 300C 3.0 CRD updated with Euro 4 compliance revisions - DPF fitment added on later 2007 units Chrysler Pacifica discontinued in UK market 2.4 World Engine begins development within DaimlerChrysler - pre-production evaluation units tested Main Engines VM Motori ENR 2.8 CRD - 2.8 turbo diesel (Grand Voyager 2.8 CRD) ECC - 2.0 DOHC petrol (Sebring 2.0, PT Cruiser 2.0 outgoing) OM640-derived 2.0 CRD - 2.0 diesel (Sebring 2.0 CRD) EZB - 5.7 Hemi V8 (300C continued) EXL 3.0 CRD - V6 diesel Euro 4 (300C Touring CRD) Popular Models Chrysler Grand Voyager (RG facelift) Chrysler 300C (LX facelift) Chrysler Sebring (JS - 2007 onward) Chrysler PT Cruiser (final years) Known For VM Motori 2.8 CRD is a well-engineered diesel - injector failure is the most common fault above 100,000 miles but the block itself is robust Sebring 2.0 CRD is a competent commuter unit but UK reception was lukewarm - build quality concerns affected resale value and now affect availability of quality second-hand engines 300C Hemi continued to attract strong enthusiast following despite fuel costs - early DPF fitment on late Euro 4 3.0 CRD units adds maintenance complexity PT Cruiser entering run-out phase - engine supply is increasingly specialist territory for rebuilders [Tags] Engine Codes Covered: VM Motori ENR (2.8 CRD), ECC, OM640 (2.0 CRD), EZB, EXL
years.cta: Compare 2006–2008 Chrysler Grand Voyager and 300C replacement engines from verified UK suppliers

years.preview: Fiat-Chrysler Alliance & World Engine Deployment 🔴 Major Engine Family Introduction
years.description: Short intro: Following Chrysler's bankruptcy and the Fiat alliance in 2009, the World Engine 2.0-litre family gained wider fitment across the UK range as Chrysler restructured its powertrain strategy under new corporate direction. Key Changes Fiat-Chrysler alliance finalised - Fiat-derived technology begins phased introduction into Chrysler platforms World Engine 2.0-litre (ECC/ECB) continues in Sebring and PT Cruiser as primary four-cylinder option Chrysler 300C 6.1 SRT-8 variant offered in UK - M-code 6.1 Hemi (ESF) in limited numbers Grand Voyager retains 2.8 CRD and 3.8 V6 - no new engine introductions, stability year Crossfire production ends - M112 V6 exits UK Chrysler lineup Main Engines ECB / ECC - 2.0 World Engine petrol (Sebring, PT Cruiser) VM Motori 2.8 CRD - diesel (Grand Voyager, continued) EZB - 5.7 Hemi V8 (300C) ESF - 6.1 Hemi V8 (300C SRT-8) 3.8 OHV V6 (EGA/EGH) - petrol (Grand Voyager 3.8) Popular Models Chrysler 300C (LX - final pre-refresh) Chrysler Grand Voyager Chrysler Sebring Chrysler 300C SRT-8 (limited UK) Known For 6.1 Hemi SRT-8 is a low-volume but high-demand unit - camshaft position sensor failures and valve spring wear are documented on high-mileage examples World Engine 2.0 in Sebring is adequate but uninspiring - oil consumption emerging as a concern on units above 80,000 miles 3.8 OHV V6 in Grand Voyager is effectively bulletproof mechanically but fuel consumption makes it a poor UK market choice Corporate instability during 2009 bankruptcy period has left some parts supply chains fragmented - sourcing quality remanufactured units for this era requires care [Tags] Engine Codes Covered: ECB, ECC, VM Motori 2.8 CRD, EZB, ESF, EGH (3.8 V6)
years.cta: Get quotes for 2009–2010 Chrysler replacement engines including Hemi and 2.8 CRD units

years.preview: Pentastar V6 Introduction, 300C Second Generation & 2.8 CRD Update 🔴 Major Engine Family Introduction
years.description: Short intro: The all-new Pentastar 3.6-litre V6 replaced the ageing 3.5 and 3.8 OHV petrol units across Chrysler's range, while the second-generation 300C arrived in UK showrooms with updated powertrains and revised diesel options. Key Changes Pentastar 3.6-litre V6 (ERB) introduced across 300C replacing the 3.5 EGG unit - major petrol architecture change Second-generation 300C (LD platform) launched in UK with 3.0 CRD V6 diesel and 3.6 Pentastar V6 petrol Grand Voyager updated with 2.8 CRD and 3.8 V6 - final generation before Chrysler brand's UK exit begins Early Pentastar (2011–2013) subject to cylinder head issues, camshaft failures, and misfires - significant warranty concern 300C SRT-8 updated with 6.4-litre Hemi Apache (ESG) for 2012 model year Main Engines ERB - 3.6 Pentastar V6 petrol (300C 3.6, Grand Voyager 3.6) EXL - 3.0 CRD V6 diesel (300C 3.0 CRD - LD generation) VM Motori 2.8 CRD - diesel (Grand Voyager 2.8 CRD) ESG - 6.4 Hemi Apache V8 (300C SRT-8 - from 2012) Popular Models Chrysler 300C (LD - 2012 onward) Chrysler 300C SRT-8 Chrysler Grand Voyager (final RT generation) Known For Early Pentastar 3.6 (2011–2013) carries well-documented cylinder head design flaw - misfire on cold start, camshaft failure, and engine stalling under light load reported across multiple platforms

years.preview: 2012 Pentastar units recorded the highest complaint volumes of any Chrysler engine in the modern era - camshaft adjuster and rocker arm failures are the leading causes of engine replacement enquiries
years.description: 3.0 CRD in the LD 300C is the most ownership-friendly UK powertrain of the generation - diesel economy suits long-distance driving but swirl flap and AdBlue system faults persist from prior generation 6.4 Hemi Apache is well-regarded by enthusiasts - broader bore and improved breathing over the 5.7 result in stronger high-rpm performance with fewer MDS-related idle issues [Tags] Engine Codes Covered: ERB, EXL, VM Motori 2.8 CRD, ESG, EZB
years.cta: Find 2011–2013 Chrysler Pentastar and 3.0 CRD replacement engines from UK engine specialists

years.preview: Pentastar Revised, TigerShark Introduction & 300C Final UK Years 🔴 Major Reliability Revision
years.description: Short intro: Chrysler issued a revised Pentastar V6 with corrected cylinder heads and introduced the 2.4-litre TigerShark MultiAir petrol unit under the Fiat Chrysler Automobiles umbrella - but the TigerShark brought its own significant reliability concerns. Key Changes Pentastar 3.6 cylinder heads redesigned (2014+) - resolves the camshaft adjuster and misfire faults that affected 2011–2013 units 2.4-litre TigerShark MultiAir (ED6) introduced in FCA-platform Chrysler models - incorporates Fiat's MultiAir2 variable valve technology TigerShark MultiAir2 piston ring failures cause excessive oil consumption - subject to class-action settlement in 2022 covering affected model years 300C continues in UK with 3.0 CRD and 3.6 Pentastar - no major engine introductions, brand focus shifting Chrysler brand begins withdrawal from UK mainstream retail - 300C is effectively the sole remaining new UK model Main Engines ERB (revised) - 3.6 Pentastar V6 petrol (300C 3.6 - revised cylinder heads) ED6 - 2.4 TigerShark MultiAir2 petrol (FCA-platform models) EXL - 3.0 CRD V6 diesel (300C 3.0 CRD, final UK years) ESG - 6.4 Hemi Apache V8 (300C SRT-8 continued) Popular Models Chrysler 300C (LD - final UK spec) Chrysler 300C SRT-8 Known For Revised Pentastar (2014+) is significantly more reliable than the 2011–2013 units - cylinder head issue resolved but oil consumption and rocker arm wear remain watchpoints above 100,000 miles TigerShark MultiAir2 2.4-litre has excellent power-to-displacement ratio but MultiAir piston ring design is a chronic failure point - oil consumption above 1 litre per 1,000 miles is the threshold that triggers specialist investigation 300C 3.0 CRD remains the most balanced UK ownership proposition - OM642-derived architecture holds up well at high mileage when AdBlue and swirl flap systems are maintained With Chrysler's UK retail footprint shrinking, finding franchise-trained specialists for this period requires searching independent FCA-network rebuilders [Tags] Engine Codes Covered: ERB (revised), ED6, EXL, ESG
years.cta: Compare 2014–2017 Chrysler 300C replacement engines - Pentastar, CRD, and Hemi units from UK rebuilders

years.preview: 300C Final Editions, Pentastar Maturity & Chrysler 300C Reintroduction 🔴 Performance Landmark
years.description: Short intro: The Chrysler 300C entered its final mainstream UK sales cycle, with the Pentastar V6 now in its most refined iteration, and a limited-run 300C SRT-spec reintroduction generating significant UK collector and enthusiast demand. Key Changes Pentastar 3.6 in mature production state - post-2014 cylinder head revisions have stabilised reliability at high mileage Chrysler 300C production officially confirmed ending globally - final 2023 model year sells out rapidly in North American and grey-import UK market 3.0 CRD (OM642 derivative) discontinued on 300C as Euro 6d compliance costs made the ageing architecture non-viable Limited-run 2023 Chrysler 300C reintroduction - 6.4 Hemi Apache (ESG) only - positioned as collector special; UK grey imports increasing Chrysler as active UK new-car franchise ends - brand enters heritage/used-car-only territory in British market Main Engines ERB (mature spec) - 3.6 Pentastar V6 petrol (300C final production) ESG - 6.4 Hemi Apache V8 (300C SRT - final edition, 2023) EZB - 5.7 Hemi V8 (grey-import 300C variants for UK enthusiasts) Popular Models Chrysler 300C (LD - final production) Chrysler 300C SRT-8 / 300C 2023 Final Edition Known For Mature Pentastar 3.6 is now a proven, well-documented engine - sourcing remanufactured units is straightforward through FCA-specialist rebuilders in the UK 6.4 Hemi Apache in final-edition 300C commands strong values - low UK volumes mean any engine failure is a high-cost event requiring specialist Hemi rebuilders With new Chrysler sales ended in UK, the entire brand's engine support structure has shifted to independent and specialist channels - warranty and supply chain quality varies considerably 5.7 and 6.4 Hemi engines remain the most searched Chrysler replacement units in the UK - strong enthusiast community keeps parts supply active [Tags] Engine Codes Covered: ERB (3.6 Pentastar final), ESG (6.4 Hemi), EZB (5.7 Hemi) Not sure which year your Chrysler was built? Enter your registration number and we'll identify the exact engine, model year, and compatible replacement options instantly - all backed by a minimum 12-month warranty from UK Chrysler specialists.
years.cta: Get quotes for 2018–2024 Chrysler 300C Pentastar and Hemi replacement engines from verified UK specialists

faq.h2: Chrysler Engine Replacement – Frequently Asked Questions
faq.intro: Quick, honest answers to the most common questions Chrysler owners ask before comparing engine replacement prices and finding a UK specialist.

items.question: How much does a Chrysler engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A Chrysler engine replacement in the UK typically costs £800–£2,200 for a used unit, £1,800–£4,500 for a reconditioned engine, or £2,500–£6,500 for a fully remanufactured unit. Fitting adds £400–£1,200 depending on model complexity—Voyager swaps are generally quicker than 300C CRD installations. We always recommend comparing at least three quotes, as prices for the same ENR or EXL engine code can vary significantly between suppliers. Compare Chrysler engine replacement prices now to find the best deal for your model.

items.question: What affects the cost of a Chrysler engine replacement?
items.answer: Four key factors affect your Chrysler engine replacement cost: engine size (2.4L petrol vs 3.0L CRD), condition (used vs reconditioned), vehicle complexity (PT Cruiser vs 300C), and whether you choose supply-only or supply-and-fit. Location matters too—London and Southeast labour rates can be 20–30% higher than regional specialists. Our comparison tool provides transparent, like-for-like quotes so you see exactly what's included. Get quotes from UK Chrysler engine specialists to compare fitting costs in your area.

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in my Chrysler?
items.answer: In most cases, yes—it's worth replacing the engine in your Chrysler if the chassis, gearbox, and bodywork are in good condition. A rebuilt ENR or ERB Pentastar engine with upgraded components often outlasts the original unit, whereas buying a replacement Voyager or 300C can cost far more. If your Chrysler holds reasonable value, engine replacement is almost always the smarter financial choice over scrapping. Use our free comparison tool to see whether a rebuilt or used Chrysler engine makes financial sense for your situation.
items.cta: Get a Chrysler engine quote now

items.question: What's included in a Chrysler engine supply and fit service?
items.answer: A Chrysler engine supply and fit service includes sourcing the correct engine code (ENR, EGM, EXL, etc.), removing your failed unit, installing the replacement with new fluids and filters, providing a workmanship warranty, disposing of the old engine, and a post-fit test drive. Fitting typically adds £400–£1,200 to the engine price, depending on model accessibility. Always confirm whether VAT, delivery, and ancillary parts are included in your quote. Compare Chrysler engine supply and fit deals from trusted UK specialists today.
items.cta: Get a Chrysler engine quote now

items.question: How long does a Chrysler engine replacement take?
items.answer: For common Chrysler models like the Voyager or PT Cruiser, a like-for-like engine swap typically takes 3–5 working days once the replacement unit is in hand. More complex installations, such as the 300C 3.0 CRD, can require 5–7 days due to tighter engine bay access. The biggest variable is engine availability—our network stocks commonly replaced codes like ENR and EDZ to minimise delays. Ask suppliers about current lead times when you request your Chrysler engine quotes.
items.cta: Get a Chrysler engine quote now

items.question: Do reconditioned Chrysler engines come with warranty?
items.answer: Yes, reconditioned Chrysler engines supplied through EngineMarket come with a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty as standard. Many rebuilt ENR, EXL or ERB units also offer optional 24-month coverage for added peace of mind. Warranty protection typically covers major internals like the crankshaft, pistons, timing components and oil pump—but always confirm exact terms directly with your chosen supplier before proceeding. Find reconditioned Chrysler engines with comprehensive warranty cover by comparing quotes today.

items.question: Should I choose a used or reconditioned Chrysler engine?
items.answer: Choose a used Chrysler engine if you need a budget-friendly, short-term solution or plan to sell the vehicle soon. Opt for a reconditioned unit if you intend to keep your Voyager, 300C or PT Cruiser long-term—reconditioned engines include new bearings, seals and upgraded parts addressing known failure points, and should deliver another 100,000+ miles with proper servicing. For failure-prone codes like the ENR 2.8 CRD, we strongly recommend reconditioned with upgrades. Compare used vs recon Chrysler engine prices to decide what's right for your budget.
items.cta: Compare used and reconditioned Chrysler engine options

items.question: Where can I find Chrysler engine specialists near me?
items.answer: EngineMarket connects you with a UK-wide network of Chrysler engine specialists, from Voyager-focused rebuilders in the Midlands to 300C CRD experts serving London and the Southeast. Simply enter your registration to match with nearby suppliers who stock your specific engine code—ENR, EGM, EXL or ERB—and offer fitting or nationwide delivery. Even if there isn't a dedicated Chrysler hub in your region, our network ensures you access vetted specialists nationwide. Find Chrysler engine specialists near you and request tailored quotes instantly.
items.cta: Get quotes from UK Chrysler engine specialists

items.question: What are the most common Chrysler engine failures?
items.answer: The most common Chrysler engine failures include: ENR 2.8 CRD turbocharger and EGR clogging (Grand Voyager), EXL 3.0 CRD intake valve carbon buildup (300C), EDZ 2.4L ignition coil and piston ring wear (PT Cruiser), EGM 3.3L oil consumption from worn rings (Voyager), and early ERB 3.6L Pentastar cylinder head porosity. These issues rarely have a cost-effective repair once symptoms appear, making replacement the most common outcome. Compare prices on replacement engines for your specific Chrysler failure type today.
items.cta: Get a Chrysler engine quote now

items.question: How can I get the best price on a Chrysler engine?
items.answer: To get the best price on a Chrysler engine, compare multiple quotes—prices for the same ENR or EXL code can vary by £1,000+ between suppliers. Consider a used engine for tight budgets, but choose reconditioned with upgraded components if you plan long-term ownership. Ask about supply-only pricing if you have a trusted local garage, and always confirm whether fitting, delivery and VAT are included in the quoted price. Get quotes from UK Chrysler engine specialists to secure the cheapest way to replace your engine.
